name: Synergy-Conditioned Conformal (SCC) — falsification test
one_line: For double perturbations, does conditioning conformal interval width on a PRIOR expectation of synergy give better CONDITIONAL coverage across the synergy spectrum than vanilla split conformal (constant width) at matched marginal coverage?
scientific_gap: arXiv:2603.02204 (perturbation-structured conformal) uses only a causal-descendant axis on SINGLE interventions. No published conformal method conditions calibration on COMBINATORIAL SYNERGY (deviation from additivity in double perturbations). This is the open wedge.
substrate: Norman 2019 (GSE133344): 105 single-gene perturbations, 131 double perturbations, all doubles have both constituent singles measured. Pseudobulk CP10K->log1p, effect = pert - control.
target: y = magnitude of the DOUBLE perturbation's transcriptional effect ||e_AB||. Predictor = ADDITIVE baseline ||e_A + e_B|| (the biologically principled null). Residual = |y - additive|.
the_conditioner: predicted synergy prior s_hat(A,B): a leakage-safe estimate of expected non-additivity available WITHOUT seeing the double — from single-perturbation properties only (e.g. cosine of the two single-effect directions; co-functional pathway overlap; sum of single magnitudes). NOTE: the TRUE synergy uses the double; s_hat must NOT.
method: SCC: isotonic sigma(s_hat) fit on calibration residuals; interval = additive_pred +/- sigma(s_hat)*Q_{1-alpha}(normalized scores). Compared to vanilla split conformal.

protocol.cv: leave-one-double-out or K-fold over the 131 doubles (K>=10)
protocol.primary_endpoint: conditional-coverage gap = max over synergy terciles |emp_coverage - 0.90|, at matched marginal ~0.90
protocol.secondary: mean interval width on high-synergy tercile (should be WIDER for SCC if it works)
protocol.null: permutation of s_hat labels across pairs, target perm p<0.01 for SCC-beats-vanilla on cond gap

prereg_falsifiers: F1: SCC does NOT beat vanilla on conditional-coverage gap (perm p>=0.01) -> synergy prior carries no calibration information -> stays ~77
F2: the leakage-safe s_hat does NOT correlate with true synergy (rho ~0) -> no predictable synergy signal to condition on -> negative
F3: SCC beats vanilla ONLY because it widens everything uniformly (no tercile-specific gain) -> reduces to a global width rescale, not adaptive

tier_rule.clears_80: SCC beats vanilla on conditional-coverage gap with perm p<0.01 AND the gain is concentrated on the high-synergy tercile AND s_hat is leakage-safe -> novel validated positive (first synergy-conditioned calibration) -> 80-83
tier_rule.stays_77: any falsifier fires -> honest negative, stays 76-79
tier_rule.honest_prior: ~40/60 (harder substrate, n=131, but genuine synergy dynamic range exists: synergy_frac 0.37-1.8)

leakage_guards: s_hat computed from SINGLES only, never the double
